briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Goodbye Visa and Mastercard: 130M Europeans switching to sovereign payment](https://www.lesnumeriques.com/banque-en-ligne/adieu-visa-et-mastercard-130-millions-d-europeens-basculent-vers-un-paiement-100-souverain-des-2026-n250918.html)** — *Hacker News* — 130 million Europeans are set to migrate to a sovereign payment network, marking a significant challenge to Visa and Mastercard's dominance on the continent.

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, highlighting the risks of uninsured fintech deposits.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and credit services.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis explores how open banking frameworks are reshaping payments competition and what it means for consumers and incumbents alike.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Feds release proposed open banking regulations](https://betakit.com/feds-release-proposed-open-banking-regulations/)** — *BetaKit* — The Canadian federal government has released its proposed open banking regulatory framework, a landmark step toward formalizing consumer-directed finance in Canada.

- **[Float announces $85-million Series C round to support AI push](https://betakit.com/float-announces-85-million-series-c-round-to-support-ai-push/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian fintech Float has raised an $85-million Series C to accelerate its AI-driven business finance platform for Canadian companies.

- **[RBC fined $4.25M over incorrect credit card statements](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/rbc-fined-credit-card-statements-9.7249811?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Canada's largest bank has been hit with a $4.25 million fine by regulators after issuing incorrect credit card statements to customers.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's government flagged screen scraping as a security risk and set an open banking implementation target, foreshadowing the regulatory progress now unfolding in 2026.

- **[Opinion: Our capital markets need to start looking for Canadian SpaceX's](https://financialpost.com/opinion/opinion-capital-markets-need-to-look-for-canadian-spacexs)** — *Financial Post* — A Financial Post opinion piece argues that Canadian capital markets must take bolder bets on high-growth startups, with direct implications for fintech venture funding in Canada.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Why advocates say "inclusive succession" is a hidden market opportunity for Canada](https://betakit.com/why-advocates-say-inclusive-succession-is-a-hidden-market-opportunity-for-canada/)** — *BetaKit* — Advocates highlight inclusive business succession as an untapped opportunity for Canadian entrepreneurs, with relevance to financial inclusion and SME ecosystem development in Atlantic Canada.

- **[Feds invest $3.2 million to support Manitoba's Black entrepreneurs](https://betakit.com/feds-invest-3-2-million-to-support-manitobas-black-entrepreneurs/)** — *BetaKit* — A federal investment to support underrepresented entrepreneurs in Manitoba signals ongoing government commitment to inclusive economic development that Atlantic Canadian fintech advocates could look to as a funding model.
